names.scientific_name: Sodium or calcium salt of [4-(α-(4-diethylaminophenyl)-5-hydroxy-2,4-disulfophenylmethylidene)-2,5-cyclohexadien-1-ylidene] diethylammonium hydroxide inner salt

natural_occurrence.description: A fully synthetic triarylmethane dye derived from petroleum-based benzene and naphthalene derivatives. Not found in nature. Produces sky-blue colour but has poor light stability (fades quickly when exposed to light).

manufacturing.description: Synthesized from benzene and naphthalene derivatives through condensation reactions with sulfonic acids. Additional reagents are added to form the triphenylmethane structure. The product is stabilized with calcium or sodium salts to achieve water solubility, yielding a dark blue powder or granules.

other_uses.pharmaceutical: Lymphangiography (medical imaging); sentinel lymph node biopsy during cancer surgery

eu.conditions: Permitted in limited food categories; rarely used in practice due to poor stability and allergy concerns

usa.notes: Never authorized for food, drugs, or cosmetics. Insufficient safety evidence.

japan.notes: Not approved for food use in Japan

australia_nz.notes: Not permitted in Australia or New Zealand

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: withdrawn (temporary ADI of 0–1 mg/kg was withdrawn in 1975; no current ADI)

regulatory_history.event: JECFA withdrew temporary ADI of 0-1 mg/kg - never finalized a permanent ADI
EFSA re-evaluation established ADI of 5 mg/kg bw/day based on chronic animal toxicity studies, despite JECFA never finalizing ADI
Medical literature increasingly favours methylene blue over Patent Blue V for lymph node procedures due to 2.7% anaphylaxis risk

commonly_found_in: Medical: Sentinel lymph node biopsy during cancer surgery

adi_context.source_note: Based on EFSA ADI of 5 mg/kg bw/day (2013). Note: JECFA withdrew its temporary ADI in 1975 and never finalized a permanent value. Despite EU approval, extremely rare in food due to poor stability and allergy concerns. Primary use is medical (lymph node procedures), not food.
